title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Production Prompt Mission
summary: A public-interest support desk. The desk needs consistent classification and next actions across normal, ambiguous, and adversarial requests. Planned artifact: Deliver Evaluated Support Triage Prompt with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Build normal, boundary, adversarial, and changed-context evaluation cases.
Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-schema-output-x5zf1u" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Schema Output into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-schema-output-x5zf1u
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Schema Output condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Schema Output applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use explicit schemas and validation when downstream code requires structured output.
